description: Reasoning-chain examples for v7.A - structured for review
instructions: Review each example. Add notes in 'reviewer_notes'. Set 'approved' to true/false. Adjust fields as needed.

video_description.setting: Professional kitchen, chef in work clothes
video_description.caption: POV: Your knees creak louder than the fridge door
actions: 1. Squats down + opens fridge door → creak sound
2. Stands up, opens/closes door only → no/faint sound
3. Squats down WITHOUT touching door → same creak sound

obvious.description: Fridge makes quiet sound, knees make loud sound - contrast/exaggeration humor
obvious.why_wrong: If it was just contrast, why show THREE separate actions? The middle action (door only) produces NO creak. This is evidence, not decoration.

correct.description: Diagnostic sequence - person systematically isolates variables to prove their body is the source
structure: Action 2: Door only = no creak (eliminates door)
correct.humor_source: Methodical, almost scientific approach to proving your own body is broken

examples.target_audience: Adults 30+ with physical ailments, people who appreciate subtle humor

review.reviewer_notes: The three actions are in the same scene, just to clarify. It's not three separate shots, but three separate moments as you've described

video_description.caption: POV: those two people are on shift together
actions: 1. Man wipes bar counter - squeaky 'scrubbing' sound heard
2. Camera pans to two colleagues (man and woman) laughing
3. Their laughter is high-pitched and squeaky

obvious.description: Man scrubs counter, colleagues laugh at something else - two separate events
obvious.why_wrong: Why does the camera pan FROM the scrub sound TO the laughing colleagues? If separate events, why show them in sequence? The camera movement CONNECTS sound to source.

correct.description: The 'scrubbing' sound IS the laugh. Colleagues' squeaky laughter sounds exactly like cloth on glass.
structure: Meta-comment: POV text implies this is recurring experience
correct.humor_source: Sound attribution misdirection - viewer's assumption (cleaning sound) is shattered by unexpected truth (laugh)

examples.target_audience: Service staff who recognize colleague quirks, anyone who appreciates sound-based humor

caption_sequence: Text 1: 'Undra varför den där kunden kollar så mycket på mig?'
Text 2: 'Han tycker nog att jag är snygg'
actions: 1. Barista looks at camera with flirty expression, covers mouth, smiles
3. Expression IMMEDIATELY shifts from flattered to shocked/annoyed

obvious.description: Customer is staring because barista is attractive - standard flirt scenario
obvious.why_wrong: Why does the 'splatt' sound exist? Why include text 3? These elements are the PUNCHLINE, not background decoration.

correct.description: Barista's internal monologue (text 1 & 2) shows her MISINTERPRETATION. She projects romantic interest. The truth: customer was watching his salad fall.
structure: False conclusion: 'He thinks I'm hot' (audience expectation confirmed)
Reaction: Immediate shift from flattered to annoyed
correct.humor_source: Crash between self-centered interpretation and embarrassing reality. Viewer SHARES the misinterpretation initially.

examples.target_audience: Anyone who has misread social signals, service staff, people who appreciate self-deprecating humor

review.reviewer_notes: No salad falls. The initial interpretation is correct. The barista is misreading the signals, and the sound in the middle is not a sallad falling, just a sudden realization and a type of 'child comic' movement (think tom & jerry) where the sound effect makes the turn more obvious/engaging. The first interpretation was pretty good.
review.suggested_changes: REMOVE FROM TRAINING SET - not a good reasoning-chain example since the obvious interpretation IS correct. Need replacement example.

dialogue: Customer: 'This tastes like a 3-year-old made it!'
Barista looks confused, walks away, returns
Barista: 'Eh... it WAS a 3-year-old who made it.'
Camera shows/implies a small child behind the counter

obvious.description: Customer exaggerates to complain. Barista acknowledges poor quality. Standard complaint humor.
obvious.why_wrong: If just a complaint, why specify '3-year-old'? Why not 'amateur' or 'beginner'? The specific age is not random - it's setup for literal payoff.

correct.description: Customer's complaint is HYPERBOLE ('tastes like a 3-year-old made it'). The twist: the hyperbole is LITERALLY TRUE. An actual child made the coffee.
structure: Exaggeration turns out to be fact
Humor from collision between figurative and literal language
correct.humor_source: 'Accidental truth' structure - when exaggeration accidentally hits reality. Same as 'worst day of my life' → something terrible actually happens.

examples.target_audience: People who appreciate wordplay, parents, service staff

review.reviewer_notes: This is a good interpretation. I would add that there is some humorous aspects of a customer describing the coffee as being made by a 5 year old (absurdist), but showing how the absurdist world the customer lives in is factual, makes the clip pop and delightful.

dialogue: Customer: 'Jag vill prata med någon högre upp.'
Staff walks away, returns with colleague
Colleague: Holding vape, coughing, looks confused/slow, movements are sluggish

obvious.description: 'Högre upp' = boss/manager (hierarchical). Customer wants to escalate.
obvious.why_wrong: If it was a manager, why show vape and coughing? These details are irrelevant for hierarchy interpretation.

alternative_interpretations.visual_evidence: Would be redundant - already mentioned boss
Vape in hand, coughing, confused look, slow movements

correct.description: Wordplay exploiting multiple meanings. Visual cues steer viewer to 'wrong' (intended) interpretation.
structure: Every visual element supports the 'high' interpretation
correct.humor_source: Joke works ONLY if viewer reads visual cues. Without them, it's just misunderstanding. With them, it's deliberate reinterpretation.

examples.target_audience: Swedish speakers who understand the wordplay, people who appreciate visual comedy

review.reviewer_notes: Just as an original song will have a remix, this concept is built on a strong theme of subversion. Other similar clips 'do' use the 'higher' to mean someone on a ladder, or one time, a muslim food shop gave a prayer mat and said 'the maghrib prayer starts in 5 minutes'. Meaning higher up would be god - the most high. In this video, it 'is' a man that looks like a stoner, so the meaning should be inffered to be this.

summary_for_training.key_lesson: Model must REJECT obvious interpretation before committing, using visual/audio evidence as verification

reviewer_overall_notes: 4/5 approved. salad-stare removed because obvious interpretation WAS correct - sound effect was just cartoon emphasis, not evidence of alternative meaning. Need replacement example for internal_monologue_misdirection pattern if we want to cover that type.
